content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of length NNN, let f(A)f(A)f(A) denote the bitwise OR of the elements of AAA.

That is, f(A)=(A1âˆ£A2âˆ£â€¦âˆ£AN)f(A) = (A_1 \mid A_2 \mid\ldots\mid A_N)f(A)=(A1â€‹âˆ£A2â€‹âˆ£â€¦âˆ£ANâ€‹), where âˆ£\midâˆ£ denotes bitwise OR.

For a fixed parameter MMM, we then define score(A,M)\text{score}(A, M)score(A,M) as follows:


You can perform the following operation at most once on AAA:

Choose two indices LLL and RRR (1â‰¤Lâ‰¤Râ‰¤N1 \le L \le R \le N1â‰¤Lâ‰¤Râ‰¤N).
Then, for each iâˆˆ[L,R]i \in [L, R]iâˆˆ[L,R], replace AiA_iAiâ€‹ with (AiâŠ•(2Mâˆ’1))(A_i \oplus (2^M - 1))(Aiâ€‹âŠ•(2Mâˆ’1)).

Here, âŠ•\oplusâŠ• denotes the bitwise XOR operation.


score(A,M)\text{score}(A, M)score(A,M) is then defined to be the maximum possible value f(A)f(A)f(A) can attain after performing the above operation at most once.
You are given two integers NNN and MMM.

Compute the sum of score(A,M)\text{score}(A, M)score(A,M) across all arrays AAA of length NNN satisfying 0â‰¤Ai<2M0 \le A_i \lt 2^M0â‰¤Aiâ€‹<2M.

The answer can be large, so find it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the sum of score(A,M)\text{score}(A, M)score(A,M) across all arrays AAA.
constraints: The sum of N2N^2N2 and the sum of M2M^2M2 over all test cases each won't exceed 300023000^230002.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: With N=1N = 1N=1 and M=1M = 1M=1, the possible arrays are [0][0][0] and [1][1][1].
Both of these arrays have a score of 111 - [1][1][1] needs no change, while for [0][0][0] the single element in it can be XOR-ed by 2Mâˆ’1=12^M - 1 = 12Mâˆ’1=1 to make it [1][1][1], which is optimal.
So, the sum of scores is 1+1=21+1 = 21+1=2.
Test case 222: N=1N = 1N=1 and M=3M = 3M=3. There are 888 arrays; those being all of [0],[1],â€¦,[7][0], [1], \ldots, [7][0],[1],â€¦,[7].
Of them, it can be verified that:

[0][0][0] and [7][7][7] have a score of 777.
[1][1][1] and [6][6][6] have a score of 666.
[2][2][2] and [5][5][5] have a score of 555.
[3][3][3] and [4][4][4] have a score of 444.
So, the sum of scores is 7+7+6+6+5+5+4+4=447+7+6+6+5+5+4+4 = 447+7+6+6+5+5+4+4=44.
